result:
Based on my research, here is the comprehensive JSON output:

```json
{
  "boss": "Waterfall Giant",
  "character": "The Silent",
  "boss_mechanics": "The Waterfall Giant is an Act 1 boss in the Underdocks biome with 250 HP (260 on higher ascensions). On turn 1 it uses Pressurize to buff itself with Steam Eruption. It then follows a repeating 5-move cycle: Stomp, Ram, Siphon, Pressure Gun, Pressure Up. Almost every move adds 3 stacks of Steam Eruption. The boss deals damage, applies Weakness and Frail debuffs, buffs its own Strength, and can heal. When reduced to 0 HP, the Giant does NOT die—it becomes invulnerable (999,999,999 HP) and detonates on the following turn, dealing damage equal to its accumulated Steam Eruption stacks. You must survive this final explosion to win. If Steam Eruption reaches 0 (e.g., via Doom), it dies without exploding.",
  "strategy": "This fight is a speed trial—the longer it lasts, the higher the final explosion damage, so you must kill the Giant fast. As Silent, prioritize Shiv burst damage with Blade Dance, Cloak and Dagger, and Finisher, or ramp Poison quickly with Deadly Poison, Catalyst, and Noxious Fumes to melt its 250 HP pool before Steam Eruption scales too high. On the killing turn, always reserve at least 1 energy and keep block cards or a defensive potion in hand so you can survive the detonation—many runs end because players spend all resources on the lethal blow. Apply Weak to the Giant before the explosion turn, as Weak reduces the detonation damage. Aim to end the fight within 5-6 turns so Steam Eruption stays around 25-30 damage, which is tankable with a couple of block cards and decent remaining HP.",
  "priority_cards": [
    "Catalyst (doubles poison stacks for rapid kill)",
    "Deadly Poison / Poisoned Stab (fast poison application)",
    "Noxious Fumes (passive poison every turn)",
    "Blade Dance (12 burst damage for 1 energy, multi-hit)",
    "Finisher (massive burst after playing many attacks)",
    "Cloak and Dagger (shivs + block in one card)",
    "Backflip (block + card draw for the detonation turn)",
    "Leg Sweep / Mirage (big block for surviving the explosion)",
    "Burst (doubles next skill — devastating with Catalyst)",
    "Acrobatics (card draw to find your key cards faster)",
    "Neutralize (applies Weak to reduce explosion damage)"
  ],
  "cards_to_avoid_playing": [
    "Slow setup powers that waste early turns without dealing damage",
    "Expensive defensive cards early in the fight (delays the kill and increases Steam Eruption)",
    "Niche combo pieces you can't activate quickly (fight is too short for long ramp-ups)",
    "Excessive deck-bloat cards that dilute draw consistency on the critical detonation turn"
  ],
  "hp_threshold": "Enter the fight with at least 50+ HP. The final explosion is unavoidable and will typically deal 25-40 damage depending on fight length. Even with Weak applied and some block, arriving at low HP means the detonation ends your run. Rest at campfires before this boss if needed—you fully heal after Act 1 bosses anyway.",
  "potion_advice": "Save your best potions for this fight—do NOT use them on hallway encounters. Use damage potions (Attack Potion, Poison Potion, etc.) early in the fight to shorten it and reduce Steam Eruption stacks. Save defensive potions (Block Potion, Dexterity Potion, Fairy in a Bottle) specifically for the explosion turn after you deliver the killing blow. The ideal sequence is: use offensive potions on your biggest burst turn to kill the Giant, then use defensive potions on the detonation turn to survive. A well-timed Block Potion on the final turn can be the difference between a win and a dead run."
}
```
